Cutting Knowledge Date: December 2023
Today Date: 26 Jul 2024

<|eot_id|><|start_header_id|>user<|end_header_id|>

Write a 30-line poem with short sentences without any comma. Each line should contain exactly one sentence. Make sure that you put the right punctuation at the end of each line. Your entire response should contain the poem only.<|eot_id|><|start_header_id|>assistant<|end_header_id|>

The sun rises high in the morning sky.
Golden rays shine down upon my face.
Birds sing sweet melodies in the trees.
Their songs echo through the empty space.
The world awakens from its sleep.
Morning dew glistens on the grass.
Fresh and clean the air feels to breathe.
Nature's beauty is a wondrous thing.
The sun's warm touch on my skin feels great.
Summer days are long and hot.
Laughter and joy fill the air.
Children play outside without a care.
Their smiles are contagious and bright.
The world is full of wonder and might.
Every day is a new surprise.
The sun sets slow and paints the sky.
Colors blend and swirl together high.
The stars come out and twinkle bright.
Night falls and the world is still.
The moon glows with a gentle light.
Shadows dance upon the wall tonight.
The world is full of mystery and might.
The unknown is a thrilling sight.
The darkness is a peaceful place.
The stars shine down with a gentle face.
The world is full of magic and wonder.
The night air is cool and fresh to ponder.
The world is a beautiful and wondrous place.
The sun rises high in the morning light.
The day begins and all is made right.
The world is full of hope and new beginnings.
The future is bright and full of
